segments.text: Hey Killian, I saw you wrote this
amazing article about hooking up cloud
code to sonar cube. What problem are we
actually trying to solve here and how?
So AI agents are really good at
generating code fast. Uh but they're not
always great about reviewing the code
that they just generated. Sometimes
they'll introduce security
vulnerabilities or they'll miss edge
cases or they'll use deprecated
patterns. Um so then that means that
you're stuck with being the cleanup crew
and going through and making sure that
everything's kosher. So the idea is is
what if Claude was able to review its
own code and the way that that happens
is we give it access to Sonar Cube cloud
through the MCP server. And that means
that the loop is is that Cloud generates
the code and then runs the scanner and
then it will verify the scan and pull in
all those rules with the MCP server and
then anything that it sees it will go
ahead and make those fixes and then
rerun the scanner. So that means that by
the time anything even gets pushed, it's
already been verified and validated.
Two files here. First is this sonar
project properties file and that's in
the project root. And so what this does
is it tells the scanner some information
about your project like the key and the
org. Uh as well as this line here which
says quality gate.weight equals true.
And what that does is it makes the
scanner block until the analysis
finishes. So Claude gets a hard pass or
fail. And the second file here is the
claude.md.
And essentially it's the behavioral
contract for Claude. And it says you
have to scan every piece of code that
you generate. If something fails, fix it
and then scan again. Don't come back to
me until the quality gate passes.
So, I have a claw CLI session loaded up
here. Um, as you can see, uh, you can
use this uh, MCP command, and it'll list
out the MCPS that you have available.
So, we see that we have the Sonar Cube
MCP available. We can go ahead and paste
in a prompt. And I'm going to ask it to
write a Python script that uploads a CSV
to S3, which is a pretty standard task,
but it has some hidden security pitfalls
that we may see. So, we'll go ahead and
hit enter and let it generate.
It's reading those two files
and it'll write the code.
Let's take a quick look at what cloud
rep. [clears throat]
So we have read CSV here
to bytes
upload function
using boto3.
Watch what happens next.
So it automatically creates a test file
as well. Take a quick look through that.
I'll approve that edit.
It's going to update this uh sonar
project properties file so that sonar is
able to read uh the test coverage when
it scans.
So we'll approve that.
And now it automatically runs the sonar
scanner. It knows that scanning is
mandatory.
This is an option that you can do to
bind it. I'm going to click no just for
now.
We'll get that sonar scan going.
It looks like the quality gate failed.
And it'll automatically get detailed
results with the MCP tool. So, it's
going to grab the status.
Can pop that open.
Okay.
Uh expected bucket owner issue. So, rule
7 uh S7608 missing bucket ownership
verification. Um, if you upload to S3
without verifying that you own the
target bucket, you could be writing data
to a bucket and attacker controls. And
this is something that, you know,
someone could easily miss in a manual
review.
Get some more information about the
rule. [snorts]
So Claude drills into the rule via the
MCP. It pulls the full documentation and
reads the compliant code example. It's
connecting the scan failure to the
actual fix without any input from me.
Look, it's already offering to go ahead
and make that fix right there. So, it
added the expected bucket owner
parameter
and
it'll iterate in making proper fixes.
and it'll update the tests as well.
So, yep, I'll approve those changes to
the tests.
I'll rerun these tests.
Awesome. Automatically reruns the sonar

Yep. So, the quality gate passes. We can
confirm that with the MCP tools again.
We can see
everything looks okay here.
Perfect. All conditions are green. And
so if I flip over
here, yep, we can see um everything's
green. The clo cod claude is handing me
spent through the same review that my CI
pipeline would run.
So it's not just generating the code.
It's accountable to the same quality
gate every developer on the team hits.
Five then verify. So we'll include the

into your project and then hook up the
MCP server and then you have an agent

Happy coding.
